reasoning: The market estimated Messi's probability to play at 0.88 as of April 2, 2026. Since then, evidence shows he returned from minor hamstring injuries in January and February to play for Argentina in their late March tune-up friendlies against Mauritania and Zambia, even starting and providing an assist against Zambia in what was billed as a farewell match on home soil. While he has publicly held back from a formal commitment—likely to give himself an out if his body fails—his active participation in the full qualifying campaign (finishing as top scorer) and the latest friendlies strongly indicate he intends to play if fit. The main risk is another soft tissue injury before or during the tournament that keeps him entirely benched, which is a real and non-negligible possibility at age 38/39. However, requiring him to play only *one* match across the tournament provides a high floor. I estimate the probability around 0.90 to account for a ~10% chance of a pre-tournament injury or last-minute withdrawal.
